# device-kit-oai

<!--
Copyright (C) 2026 Work Louder Inc. - All Rights Reserved
Unauthorized copying of this file, via any medium is strictly prohibited
Proprietary and confidential

Written by Cristian Duciuc <cristian@worklouder.cc>, March 2026
-->

Electron.js library for discovering, connecting to, and controlling Work Louder hardware (keyboards, macro pads, and knobs) over USB HID and serial. Provides device status queries, lighting previews, and OpenAI-specific lighting RPCs (per-thread state and keys/ambient configuration).

## Requirements

- Node.js >= 18
- Yarn 1.x (classic)
- **macOS Input Monitoring permission** — The library opens HID connections in non-exclusive mode, which requires the host application (e.g. Electron, Terminal) to be granted **Input Monitoring** access. Go to **System Settings → Privacy & Security → Input Monitoring** and enable the app that will run this library. Without this permission, HID device communication will fail silently or be rejected by the OS.

## Install

This is a private package hosted on [GitHub Packages](https://github.com/worklouder/device-kit-oai/packages). Authenticate with a GitHub personal access token that has `read:packages` scope, then install:

```bash
# .npmrc (project or user level)
@worklouder:registry=https://npm.pkg.github.com
//npm.pkg.github.com/:_authToken=${GITHUB_TOKEN}
```

```bash
yarn add @worklouder/device-kit-oai
```

## Build

```bash
yarn install
yarn build     # compiles TypeScript to dist/
```

## Architecture

```
src/
├── rpc_api_oai/  # High-level typed API (status, lighting, notifications)
└── index.ts      # Public surface — re-exports discovery, comm, types
```

| Module | Entry class | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| **Device Discovery** | `WLDeviceDiscovery` | Scan USB for connected Work Louder devices and bootloaders |
| **Device Comm** | `WLDeviceCommImpl` | Open a transport connection (HID or serial), manage the send queue |
| **RPC API** | `RPCApiOAI` | High-level typed methods — device status, lighting (preview, threads, keys/ambient config), and notifications |

## Quick start

```ts
import {
  WLDeviceDiscovery,
  WLDeviceCommImpl,
  RPCApiOAI,
  ConnectionEventType,
} from "@worklouder/device-kit-oai";

// 1. Discover devices
const discovery = new WLDeviceDiscovery();
const devices = discovery.findWLDevices();

if (devices.length === 0) {
  console.log("No devices found");
  process.exit(1);
}

// 2. Connect
const comm = new WLDeviceCommImpl();

comm.onConnectionEvent((event) => {
  if (event.type === ConnectionEventType.DISCONNECTED) {
    console.log("Device disconnected");
  }
});

await comm.connect(devices[0]);

// 3. Use the API
const api = new RPCApiOAI(comm);

const status = await api.getDeviceStatus();
console.log("Firmware:", status.firmwareVersion);
console.log("Battery:", status.batteryPercentage, "%");

// 4. Disconnect when done
await comm.disconnect();
```

## Device discovery

```ts
const discovery = new WLDeviceDiscovery();

// All Work Louder HID devices
const all = discovery.findWLDevices();

// Filter by product type
import { DeviceType } from "@worklouder/device-kit-oai";
const knobs = discovery.findWLDevices([DeviceType.Knob]);

```

## Connection events

Subscribe to transport lifecycle changes on the `WLDeviceCommImpl` instance:

```ts
const unsub = comm.onConnectionEvent((event) => {
  switch (event.type) {
    case ConnectionEventType.CONNECTED:
      console.log("Connected");
      break;
    case ConnectionEventType.DISCONNECTED:
      console.log("Disconnected");
      break;
    case ConnectionEventType.ERROR:
      console.error("Error:", event.error);
      break;
  }
});

// Stop listening
unsub();
```

## Device notifications

Subscribe to device notifications through `RPCApiOAI`:

```ts
import { RPCApiOAI } from "@worklouder/device-kit-oai";

const api = new RPCApiOAI(comm);

const unsubscribeHid = api.onHidReceived((hid) => {
  console.log("HID event:", hid);
});

const unsubscribeJoystick = api.onJoystickMove((pos) => {
  console.log("Joystick move:", pos);
});

// Stop listening when no longer needed
unsubscribeHid();
unsubscribeJoystick();
```

## API reference

### `RPCApiOAI`

#### `getFirmwareVersion(): Promise<string>`

Returns the semantic version string of the running firmware (e.g. `"1.2.3"`).

#### `getDeviceStatus(): Promise<WLDeviceStatus>`

Returns a snapshot of the current device state:

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `firmwareVersion` | `string?` | Semantic version of the running firmware |
| `selectedProfileIndex` | `number?` | Zero-based index of the active profile |
| `selectedLayerIndex` | `number?` | Zero-based index of the active layer within the profile |
| `batteryPercentage` | `number?` | Battery charge level 0–100 (wireless devices only) |
| `isCharging` | `boolean?` | `true` if charging via USB |

#### `sendLightingPreview(config: WLDeviceLightingConfig): Promise<void>`

Sends a real-time lighting preview to the device. The config has two zones — `backlight` and `underglow` — each a `SideLightingConfig`:

```ts
import { LightingEffect } from "@worklouder/device-kit-oai";
import type { WLDeviceLightingConfig } from "@worklouder/device-kit-oai";

const config: WLDeviceLightingConfig = {
  backlight: {
    effect: LightingEffect.solid,
    brightness: 0.8,
    color: 0xFF5500, // packed RGB int32
  },
  underglow: {
    effect: LightingEffect.rainbow,
    speed: 0.5,
    brightness: 1.0,
  },
};

await api.sendLightingPreview(config);
```

**`SideLightingConfig` fields:**

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `effect` | `LightingEffect` | Animation effect to apply |
| `brightness` | `number?` | Intensity, normalized 0–1 |
| `speed` | `number?` | Effect speed, normalized 0–1 |
| `magic` | `number?` | Effect-specific parameter, normalized 0–1 |
| `color` | `number?` | Packed RGB integer (e.g. `0xFF0000` for red) |

**`LightingEffect` values (used with `sendLightingPreview` only):** `off`, `solid`, `snake`, `rainbow`, `breath`, `gradient`

#### `sendThreadsLighting(threads: ThreadLighting[]): Promise<boolean>`

Sends per-thread lighting on the device. The payload is an
array with one entry per thread. **Only `id` is required** on each entry; omit
any other property when you want that attribute left unchanged.

Returns `true` if the call succeeds, or `false` if it throws (errors are logged via the optional `Logger`).

```ts
import {
  OAILightingEffect,
  type ThreadLighting,
} from "@worklouder/device-kit-oai";

const threads: ThreadLighting[] = [
  {
    id: 0,
    color: 0xff5500,
    brightness: 1,
    effect: OAILightingEffect.solid,
    speed: 0,
    syncKeysLighting: true,
    syncAmbientLighting: false,
  },
  { id: 1, brightness: 0.5 },
  { id: 2, effect: OAILightingEffect.shallowBreath, speed: 0.4 },
];

const ok = await api.sendThreadsLighting(threads);
```

**`ThreadLighting` fields:**

| Field | Description |
|---|---|
| `id` | Which thread to update (required). |
| `color` | Packed RGB color (integer). |
| `brightness` | Intensity from 0 (off) through 1 (full on). |
| `effect` | Animation; see `OAILightingEffect`. |
| `speed` | Effect speed from 0 (stopped) through 1 (fast). |
| `syncKeysLighting` | Keys backlight follows this thread's RGB when set. |
| `syncAmbientLighting` | Ambient ring follows this thread's RGB when set. |

**`OAILightingEffect` values:** `off`, `solid`, `snake`, `rainbow`, `breath`, `gradient`, `shallowBreath`. Numeric codes 0–5 match `LightingEffect` on the base kit; `shallowBreath` is code 6 (breathing pattern from half brightness to full brightness).

#### `sendLightingConfig(config: OAILightingConfig): Promise<boolean>`

Applies `OAILightingSide` settings for the outer ambient ring (`ambient`) and
the lighting under the keycaps (`keys`).

Returns `true` if the call succeeds, or `false` if it throws.

```ts
import {
  OAILightingEffect,
  type OAILightingConfig,
} from "@worklouder/device-kit-oai";

const config: OAILightingConfig = {
  keys: {
    effect: OAILightingEffect.shallowBreath,
    brightness: 0.9,
    speed: 0.4,
    magic: 0,
    color: 0x2244ff,
  },
  ambient: {
    effect: OAILightingEffect.rainbow,
    brightness: 0.7,
    speed: 0.5,
    magic: 0,
    color: 0,
  },
};

const ok = await api.sendLightingConfig(config);
```

**`OAILightingSide` fields:**

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `effect` | `OAILightingEffect` | Animation effect |
| `brightness` | `number` | Intensity 0–1 |
| `speed` | `number` | Effect speed 0–1 |
| `magic` | `number` | Effect-specific parameter 0–1 |
| `color` | `number` | Packed RGB integer |

#### `onHidReceived(callback: (hid: HIDKey) => void): () => void`

Subscribes to custom HID key events from the device. Returns an unsubscribe function.

**`HIDKey` fields:**

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `key` | `string` | The HID key identifier |
| `act` | `number?` | Action type like press and release |
| `agent` | `number?` | Agent index that triggered the event |

#### `onJoystickMove(callback: (pos: JoystickPos) => void): () => void`

Subscribes to joystick movement notifications. Returns an unsubscribe function.

**`JoystickPos` fields:**

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `angle` | `number` | Joystick angle, normalized 0–1 |
| `distance` | `number` | Joystick displacement from center, normalized 0–1 |

## Custom logger

All constructors accept an optional `Logger` object. If omitted, a silent no-op logger is used and no diagnostic output is produced:

```ts
const logger = {
  info: (...args: any[]) => myLogger.info(...args),
  error: (...args: any[]) => myLogger.error(...args),
  debug: (...args: any[]) => myLogger.debug(...args),
  warn: (...args: any[]) => myLogger.warn(...args),
};

const discovery = new WLDeviceDiscovery(logger);
const comm = new WLDeviceCommImpl(logger);
const api = new RPCApiOAI(comm, logger);
```
